text:
Once upon a time in a small forest clearing lived a wise old owl. Every morning he'd flap his wings and whistle a cheerful tune. The animals loved his music. One day a shy squirrel whispered a dream.

Dreams! Owls often dreamed of flight. Maybe he could soar through the stars? The squirrel scurried to an elder.

A lifetime of dreams. You're a night owl. So dream dreams.

With a wink and a nod the elder flew away.

A week passed. The squirrel soared dreamily.

Then shadow swallowed light.

The owl wwoke. Shadows had feet. They came knocking.

"Who's there? What business?" Owls didn'd fear birds.

Just an old owl counting stars.

Oh no! Stars dwindled.

Count them again. Maybe nine.

The big owl swooped. Night fell. Stars gleamed.

Now six!

Asleep. Rest easy. Dreamers dream.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
